message:
fix(security): zero recycled symbol pool buffers to prevent stale payload leakage

Both SymbolPool implementations (src/types/resource.rs and
src/util/resource.rs) now fill deallocated buffers with zeros before
returning them to the free list. Previously, a recycled buffer retained
the prior caller's payload bytes, which could leak sensitive data if
the buffer was reallocated to a different task or region.

The zeroing happens before mark_free/clear_checkout_state so the buffer
is clean by the time it becomes available for reuse.

Tests added in both modules: pool_reuses_zeroed_buffers_after_deallocate
fills a buffer with 0xAA, deallocates it, reallocates from the pool,
and asserts all bytes are zero.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
